titleStmt.title: Letter from Jerome J. Ronayne to Margaret O'Brien, 14 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Jerome J. Ronayne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This is a letter from Jerome J. Ronayne, town clerk, Urban District of Midleton, County Cork, Ireland, to Margaret O'Brien.
Mrs. O'Brien has applied for permission to move her deceased husband's remains from one grave to another within the Protestant church graveyard. As sanitation authority for the area, the Midleton Urban District Council approves the move, provided she gets the consent of the church authorities.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/10140/1

correspAction.placeName: The Green, Midleton, County Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
JEROME J. RONAYNE
TOWN CLERK Urban District of Midleton,
CO. CORK, 14th. day of June 1916 Mrs. Margaret O'Brien,
The Green,
MIDLETON Dear Madam/ With reference to your application for
permission to remove the remains of your husband
from one grave to another in the burial ground
attached to the Protestant Church, I am directed
by the Midleton Urban District Council to inform
you that they, as Sanitary Authority for the
District, express their consent to this course,
on the understanding that you are able to obtain the
consent of the church Authorities. Yours faithfully Jerome J. Roynane I. Instruct police not to interfere
with Mrs. O'Brien or her agents
when engaged in the transfer
of the remains II. Inform Mrs. OBrien. III. —
EOF
16:6:16
I.II done 07/6
